issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for placeholder text
issuesFound.explanation: The placeholder text uses var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.1:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making it difficult for users with low vision to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 220: `input::placeholder, textarea::placeholder { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); opacity: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset, which does not create a visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 320: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for section header paragraph text
issuesFound.explanation: The section header paragraph uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 530: `.section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin-top: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size for search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, which falls between 24px and 43px with no compensating padding to reach the recommended 44px touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 915: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the minimum 24px touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to select the checkbox accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset without setting a visible outline width or alternative such as box-shadow. Keyboard users cannot see the focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: `.focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only changes outline-color. With outline-width remaining at zero, no visible focus ring renders for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 339: `.focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus state removes the default outline with outline: none and only sets outline-offset without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 626: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes transform in its transition property without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience motion sickness from the transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 631: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover state applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) without a prefers-reduced-motion override. Users sensitive to motion may experience discomfort from this animation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible outline color, box-shadow, or border. This makes keyboard focus invisible for all elements relying on this global rule, failing WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in Forced Colors Mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible without providing a solid outline color. In High Contrast Mode, box-shadows are ignored, so this renders the focus indicator completely invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline and applies an offset but does not define an outline color or visible border. Keyboard users will not see focus on buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus selector removes the outline and relies solely on box-shadow. Box-shadows are not visible in Windows High Contrast Mode, making focus invisible for users relying on that mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an offset. No visible color or border is defined, hiding focus from keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the outline without providing a visible alternative color or border. Focus is invisible in this component.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline with only an offset. No visible indicator remains for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus in this nav region.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 44px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum and far below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-height: 20px and min-width: 20px. Icon buttons must be at least 24px, preferably 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav toggle button has min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. It effectively does nothing, potentially exposing screen-reader-only text to sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties. There is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement for users who request reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.9:1
issuesFound.explanation: Input hint text uses #aaaaaa on white background, making it hard to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. Body text links (e.g., inside .prose p) lose their underline and rely solely on color, failing SC 1.4.1.

issuesFound.fix: Line 359: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 409 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transform transition, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override disabling this specific motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 409: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform (rotate) and transition, but the prefers-reduced-motion media query blocks at Line 847 are empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line 847: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes the outline entirely with outline: none and provides no visible alternative. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, so outline is the only reliable focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline. This removes the default focus ring for all keyboard-focusable elements, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a

issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator in Windows High Contrast Mode where box-shadow is suppressed.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px without providing a visible focus indicator replacement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on code elements
issuesFound.explanation: The code element uses color: var(--colour-brand-secondary) which is #bb88ee against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100) which is #f1f5f9, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.75:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links set min-height: 24px with no padding compensation, meeting the 24px minimum but falling below the 44px recommended touch target size without adequate spacing.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the base rule and only changes outline-color without setting a non-zero outline width, resulting in no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, clip: auto, and auto dimensions, making the content visible instead of hidden. It fails to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a 28px touch target that is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size without sufficient padding to compensate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes transition properties affecting transform (translateY) without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override, which can cause vestibular issues for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox inputs set width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size, making them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links inherit text-decoration: none from the global rule and only provide color change on hover without additional non-colour visual differentiators, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for colour-blind users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function marks form fields as invalid by linking error messages via aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Assistive technologies cannot identify which fields have failed validation, affecting all forms using this utility (newsletter, contact, wizard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1426: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after the existing `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens by adding CSS classes and removing the hidden attribute, but the function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the navigation menu, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2328: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after setting `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the panel is displayed and CSS classes are toggled, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true". This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2467: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item is expanded, the function animates the panel open and updates CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates the WAI-ARIA Accordion pattern and prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4131: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer declares a focus trap variable (`_trap`) but never initializes or activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the open navigation drawer and become lost in the background content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2332: After `_open = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { initialFocus: getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] }); _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not notified that the menu has closed, degrading the experience for assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the dropdown state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function opens the dropdown but does not announce the action to screen readers via a live region. Users are not notified that the menu has opened, significantly degrading the experience.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function closes the dropdown but does not announce the action to screen readers via a live region. Users are not notified that the menu has closed, significantly degrading the experience.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the FAQ answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the answer is now expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the FAQ answer panel but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not notified that the answer has collapsed, degrading the experience.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, violating WCAG 3.3.1.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onAccept function does not announce result to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onAccept function processes user consent but does not announce the success message to screen readers via a live region. Users are not notified that their preferences were saved, degrading the experience.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onDecline function does not announce result to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onDecline function processes user consent but does not announce the success message to screen readers via a live region. Users are not notified that their preferences were saved, degrading the experience.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles CSS classes to show the navigation drawer and locks scroll, but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button from "false" to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2155: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after setting `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap() or activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the open mobile menu into the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2156 (inside open function): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` and corresponding cleanup in close()

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the mobile navigation, the function updates aria-expanded and CSS classes but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the liveRegion utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2185: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before the emit call

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 for state management.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2285: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance before or after the emit call

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu updates the DOM and ARIA states but provides no auditory feedback to screen reader users that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2305: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');` or similar before the emit call

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the FAQ panel and animates the height, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button to indicate the expanded state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3428: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: While opening an FAQ item announces via liveRegion, closing it does not, leaving screen reader users unaware that the content has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3458: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` before the emit call

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause missing state announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The play() and pause() functions update aria-pressed and aria-label on the button, but do not announce the auto-play state change (playing/paused) via the liveRegion, leaving users unaware if the carousel is rotating automatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3608 (in play): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselPlaying || 'Carousel auto-play started');` and Line 3618 (in pause): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselPaused || 'Carousel auto-play paused');`

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent accept/decline missing confirmation announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users accept or decline cookies, the banner is hidden immediately without announcing the action result to screen readers, leaving users uncertain if their choice was registered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4158 (_onAccept): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookiesAccepted || 'Cookie preferences saved: Analytics enabled');` and Line 4164 (_onDecline): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookiesDeclined || 'Cookie preferences saved: Essential cookies only');`

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher init missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: While the set() function updates aria-pressed on theme toggle buttons, the init() function applies the saved theme without initializing aria-pressed on the toggle buttons, leaving them in an indeterminate state until first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4075 (in init, after _apply): Add the loop from set() to initialize aria-pressed: `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { const btnTheme = btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle'); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnTheme === _current)); });`

The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function for marking fields invalid sets aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markInvalid function: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle functions modify CSS classes to show/hide the drawer but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open function: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation does not trigger a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not notified when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open function: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions toggle visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the dropdown is currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: dropdownMenu._openInstance function: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu does not announce the change to a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line: dropdownMenu._openInstance function: liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the trigger element is not initialised with aria-expanded="false". The initial state is unknown to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: dropdownMenu.register function: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.fix: Line: faqAccordion.open function: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));

issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markValid function: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function sets the panel visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: faqAccordion.open function: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the validator adds error styling and descriptions but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. When validation passes, it does not set aria-invalid="false". Screen readers rely on this attribute to convey field validity to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _renderResult function (invalid branch): add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`. Line: _renderResult function (valid branch): add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu instance open and close functions manage panel visibility and CSS classes, but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the panel's visible state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _openInstance function: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line: _closeInstance function: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `if (!inst.open) return;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price values update visually but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users will not be informed that the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: setPeriod function (end of function, after emit): add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds the open CSS class, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close function sets aria-expanded="false", the open function must set it to "true" to maintain state consistency.
issuesFound.fix: Line: open function: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the page layout significantly, but these state changes are not announced to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line: open function: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`. Line: close function: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) cycle between active and inactive states but do not set the aria-pressed attribute to reflect which button is currently pressed. This prevents screen reader users from understanding which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _syncToggleState function: after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The site search suggestion panel open and close functions manage the _searchState.open boolean and show/hide the listbox, but never update aria-expanded on the search input combobox to indicate whether the suggestions are visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _open function: add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line: _close function: add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads with the suggestion panel closed. The aria-expanded attribute must reflect the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: init function (after _initialised = true): add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads and the menu is in the closed state. The aria-expanded attribute must reflect the initial state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line: init function (after _initialised = true): add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks an aria-controls attribute establishing the programmatic relationship with the drawer element it expands and collapses.
issuesFound.fix: Line: init function (after _initialised = true): add `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = srUtils.generateId('mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content, but these state changes are not announced to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _openInstance function: add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`. Line: _closeInstance function: add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger. (Note: close sets it to false, but open does not set it to true).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown menu, the trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" even though the panel is hidden by default. The aria-expanded attribute must be present on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line: register function (after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing FAQ items changes the visible content, but these actions are not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line: open function: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`. Line: close function: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`.

issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the drawer visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function sets up event listeners but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button. The initial state of the menu is not conveyed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but does not update aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Assistive technology users are not notified of the state change.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._hideBanner does not restore background from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The _hideBanner function closes the modal but does not remove aria-hidden="true" from the main content. The page remains inaccessible to screen readers after closing.

issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to indicate validation errors but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state to users, preventing them from understanding when form fields contain errors.

issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons function as a toggle group where one button is always selected (activeFilter), but they lack `aria-pressed`. Screen readers cannot convey which filter is currently active.

>

issuesFound.title: Skip links navigation missing accessible name
